terms.note: May be imitation/benzaldehyde; verify if true almond

Dairy butter; guard nut/plant butter uses in matcher.
Dairy cheese; guard vegan/non-dairy uses in matcher.
Dairy cream; guard plant/non-dairy uses in matcher.
In cheese/dairy products may carry milk.
Usually fermented from sugar; occasionally dairy. Verify.
Pure lactose is a milk SUGAR; may carry trace milk protein depending on manufacture. Also an intolerance parent.

CPP-ACP; milk-derived ingredient in some dental/gum products
Often shellfish-derived; can be corn-fermented — verify source
Highly refined peanut oil is exempt from major-allergen labeling (statute targets allergenic protein); unrefined/cold-/expeller-pressed retains protein.
Unrefined — retains allergenic protein; not exempt.
Unrefined — retains allergenic protein; not exempt.
Refined groundnut oil is protein-exempt; unrefined retains protein.
Refined is largely protein-exempt; unrefined retains protein.
Usually contains tahini (sesame) but not always; broad term — inference only.
Refined sesame oil is largely protein-exempt; culinary/toasted sesame oil is usually unrefined and retains protein.

Emulsifier; can be soy-, other-plant-, or animal-derived.
Soy-derived emulsifier (E322); many soy-allergic tolerate it

Highly refined oil often tolerated; cold-pressed may carry protein
Often soy-derived; can be sunflower/other. Verify.

almond.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare almond whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Almond butter, Almond flour or Almond paste.

brazil_nut.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare brazil nut whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Brazil nuts.

cashew.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare cashew whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Anacardium, Cashew butter or Cashews.

crustacean.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare crustacean shellfish whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Crab, Crawfish or Crayfish.

egg.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare eggs whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Albumen, Albumin or Apovitellin.

fish.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare fish whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Anchovy, Bass or Bottarga.

hazelnut.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare hazelnut whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Filbert, Gianduja or Hazelnuts.

macadamia.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare macadamia whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Bush nut, Macadamia nut or Queensland nut.

milk.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare milk and dairy whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Buffalo milk, Butter or Butterfat.

mollusc.info: U.S. labels aren't required to call out mollusc shellfish, so it can be easy to miss in an ingredient list. On labels it can also show up as Abalone, Calamari or Clam.

peanut.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare peanuts whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Arachis hypogaea, Beer nuts or Cold-pressed peanut oil.

pecan.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare pecan whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Pecans.

pine_nut.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare pine nut whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Pignoli, Pine nuts or Pinon.

pistachio.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare pistachio whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Pistachios.

sesame.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare sesame whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Ajonjoli, Benne or Benniseed.

soy.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare soybeans whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Edamame, Hydrolyzed soy protein or Kinako.

walnut.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare walnut whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Black walnut, California walnut or English walnut.

wheat.info: U.S. food labels are required to declare wheat whenever it's an ingredient. On labels it can also show up as Atta, Atta flour or Bulgur.

caffeine.info: Many people find caffeine hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Black tea, Coffee or Green tea.

fodmaps.info: Many people find fodmaps hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Chicory root, Fructans or GOS.

fructose.info: Many people find fructose hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Agave, Agave nectar or Crystalline fructose.

gluten.info: Many people find gluten hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Barley, Bulgur or Durum.

histamine.info: Many people find histamine hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Aged cheese, Cured meats or Fermented foods.

lactose.info: Many people find lactose hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Buttermilk, Condensed milk or Cream.

sulfites.info: Many people find sulfites hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as E220, E223 or E224.

sweeteners.info: Many people find artificial sweeteners hard on their body. It isn't an allergy, but the discomfort is real — so we watch for it just as carefully. On labels it can also show up as Acesulfame K, Acesulfame potassium or Advantame.
